entry.title: Recurring Deduction Integration in SAP EC: Challenges, Limitations & Workaround
entry.content:
Introduction

In one of our recent Employee Central implementations, we encountered a requirement to integrate data from a custom MDF object into the standard Recurring Deduction object. While this initially appeared to be a straightforward integration scenario, further analysis revealed system limitations that required a different approach. This blog outlines the challenge, solution design, and key learnings from this experience.

Business Requirement

The requirement was to: Extract data from a custom MDF object

Push the data into the Recurring Deduction object

Ensure timely processing aligned with payroll needs

Challenges Faced

The initial approach was to use standard Employee Central APIs.

However, during analysis, we identified a key limitation: the Recurring Deduction object does not have API visibility enabled in Object Definition.

We explored whether standard upsert mechanisms could be used as a workaround. However, the issue was not related to payload or mapping—it was due to the object not being exposed via API.

Solution Design

Given this limitation, we redesigned the integration approach instead of forcing a direct API-based solution.

The final design included SAP CPI and SFTP-based processing:

Data was extracted from MDF and sent to CPI

CPI transformed the data and placed it in an SFTP location

The file was then picked and processed into the Recurring Deduction object

Implementation Approach

To support this design, we implemented a two-job mechanism:

Job 1 (CPI): Extract MDF data and post it to the SFTP folder

Job 2 (Provisioning Job): Pick the file from SFTP and post it into the Recurring Deduction object

Additional Challenge

The client required the data to be available immediately (near real-time).

While SAP CPI allows multiple executions per day, the Provisioning job responsible for processing files from SFTP does not support the same level of scheduling flexibility.

Initially, this appeared to be a limitation that could impact the requirement.

Workaround Implemented

To meet the requirement, we implemented the following approach:

Created multiple identical Provisioning jobs (5 jobs)

Scheduled them at different intervals (staggered hourly)

Aligned CPI execution timing to ensure file readiness

Additionally, we maintained a "Modified Since Last Run" filter during data extraction. This ensured that only incremental data was processed and prevented duplicate records.

Outcome

With this approach:

Data was processed multiple times a day

Near real-time expectation was achieved

Duplicate records were avoided

The solution remained stable and aligned with system capabilities

Key Learnings

Not all Employee Central objects are API-enabled

System limitations should guide integration design

Direct approaches are not always feasible or optimal

Practical workarounds can effectively meet business needs

Incremental data handling is critical to avoid duplication

Conclusion

This scenario reinforced an important lesson: successful integration design is not just about technical capability, but about aligning with how the system is designed to operate.

Understanding these boundaries helps in building solutions that are both effective and sustainable.

entry.title: Q1 2026 Innovations: SAP Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
Register for our Q1 2026 Service Solutions webcast

2026 will prove to be a transformational year for many companies as AI assistants and agents drive high value in service delivery. I’m already excited about the Q2 Innovations blog! Get registered and ready for a preview at Sapphire 2026 of how SAP’s embedded AI innovations are delivering value for customers every day. But first things first, let me highlight some key innovations delivered in Q1 for SAP Enterprise Service Management (SAP ESM).

Ensuring seamless look and feel throughout a unified workspace

Delivering the right user experience has high impact on value and adoption for service specialists especially when the ESM workspace includes third party applications and processes. Using mashups is the preferred method to create an integrated environment ensuring service representatives don’t have to login to different systems or open sperate windows. Now the configuration for mashups has been extended to include theme parameters. With this setting the embedded mashup will adjust automatically to the system set theme providing a consistent look and feel for SAP and 3rd party embedded solutions.

Deliver connected experiences and activity across solutions

The event hub service in BTP allows solutions to publish inbound and outbound events ensuring others can access these activities for visibility and execution of line of business specific processes. SAP ESM now publishes outbound events to the SAP BTP Cloud Application Event Hub so other applications  receive them ensuring a unified and transparent experience across systems. This simplifies the need for system-to-system integration and centralizes events. This transparency can be realized with SAP SuccessFactors ensuring case updates are provided on employee service interactions directly on the SuccessFactors home page.

Self-Service available anytime, anywhere

Employees can get instant answers and create cases from the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app providing easy 24/7 access for HR support. This provides greater flexibility and ease of access for HR related inquiries and gives employees the ability to work from their channel of choice, even if they are on the go. HR Case management is available as well, allowing creation of new cases, viewing and updating existing cases from wherever they are. Additionally, employees receive notifications when action is needed, ensuring case resolution without response delays.

Accelerating case resolution with UX simplicity

An approval process can delay case resolutions. Working with our customers’ inputs, several improvements have been made that reduce time, ensure consistency and enable a more streamlined handling of cases where approvals are needed. Ensuring quick steps to final resolution, both Service reps and managers benefit from a more efficient process and consistency while approvals are in progress. The following capabilities have been enabled to accelerate the approval process:

  • Approval steps enabled in auto flow scenarios
  • Case status triggers notifications or email
  • Directly approve from case header
  • Easy access to visibility in next steps and status
  • Read-only case state during approval steps


Flexing AI to accelerate case resolution

Another key improvement in Q1 includes extending the business information extraction capability to include custom fields. This capability saves time, reduces errors and delivers ease to service teams – eliminating the need to scour through email for relevant information and then having to make several manual entries. SAP to SAP solutions is rather logical, but often there are custom fields or 3rd party solutions. Now with custom fields included there’s additional flexibility ensuring service reps have improved automation, prepossessing and context to quickly close cases.

 
 

 

More Information & Thank you!

For additional Q1 innovations relevant for SAP Enterprise Service Management, reference Kirsi Tarvainen’s blog on SAP Service Cloud and register for the upcoming Q1 Release Webinar, and to keep up as we release capabilities each month reference the What’s New in our help portal. Please also reference these valuable links for more on SAP’s CX Customer Service Solutions:

SAP learning ESM Course

SAP Service Cloud V2 Certification Course

SAP Sales & Service Cloud Innovation Spotlight Webinar

Release Navigator

To learn more about our Q1/2026 innovations across SAP’s CX solutions check out the blog!

Use the SAP Enterprise Service Management managed tag when posting on SAP ESM topics!

Finally, an enormous thank you to this expanding community, for your active participation and collaboration; as we all benefit – SAP employees, partners and customers -  from sharing our experiences and information on SAP ESM.

entry.title: How to Display Position Code in Latest People Profile Header – SAP SuccessFactors EC Workaround
entry.content:
In the legacy People Profile of SAP SuccessFactors, the position code was displayed alongside the position name, making it easy for consultants and HR users to identify positions quickly.

Legacy people profile – Earlier position code is available beside of the position name

However, in the latest People Profile (introduced after last year's upgrades), only the position name is displayed. The position code is not visible in the profile, making it time-consuming for users to retrieve this information from the Position Org Chart.

Latest people profile- Only the position name is displayed, and the position code is not visible in the header.

Additionally, all codes can be enabled in the People Profile using the option below; however, this will result in all codes being displayed for every user, including those that may not be relevant or required.
Latest People Profile > General Settings > Show External Codes for all Users

Requirement: Display only the position code in the People Profile header, ensuring that no additional or unnecessary codes are shown. To address the business requirement of displaying only the position code in the header, I explored a workaround that leverages configurable fields within the latest People Profile to enable the position code to be shown in the header.

Workaround Solution:

Step 1: Go to configure latest people profile > General settings

Step 2: Click on configure header fields

Step 3: Select first employee information type as Position

If Position Field Is Not Available in Dropdown - It needs to be synced to a UDF using HRIS Sync Mapping., For mapping Position field the UDF please follow the below steps.
3.1. Go to the Manage Business Configuration > Job Information > Position Field > Click on details

3.2. Add an HRIS Sync Mapping for the Position field. Map it to an unused standard element field ; for example, Customizable Field 7 can be used for this purpose. Once the mapping is done, click on Save.

3.3. Go to HRIS sync mapping check your added field weather is appearing or not.

3.4. Once HRIS Sync job will run the field data automatically available in the UDF. After that you can use this created Customizable field 7 “Position” in configure header fields.

Step 4: Save the general settings

Step 5: Navigate to the Employee Profile and Verify that the Position Code is now displayed along with the Position Name in the header

Conclusion

This workaround enables visibility of the position code in the latest People Profile header by leveraging header field configuration and HRIS Sync Mapping. As a result, HR users and consultants can quickly identify positions without navigating to the Position Org Chart, improving efficiency and overall user experience.

entry.title: CSV Validator App Is Now Open Source
entry.content:
From Solution to Community Tool

A few weeks ago, I shared how CSVValidationApp helps solve data quality issues in SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub. The response surprised me. Many have built similar workarounds, spent hours cleaning data manually, or watched perfectly good imports fail because of stray HTML tags.

That's when I felt this shouldn't be "my tool." It should be our tool.

Today, CSV Validator App is officially open source and available on GitHub under the Apache 2.0 license: github.com/SAP/csv-validator

Why This Matters

I didn't build this tool because I love CSV files (I don't). I built it because I was tired of seeing good work derailed by preventable issues. An HR team prepares a skills library update, exports clean data, imports it into TIH—and suddenly descriptions are broken, special characters are corrupted, and they're troubleshooting instead of enabling their workforce.

It's frustrating. And it's common.

Making this open source isn't about recognition or downloads. It's about honesty: I don't have all the answers. You might be importing recruiting data with different quirks. You might support a region with specific character encoding needs. You might have found a validation rule I missed.

By opening the code, we can solve these problems together—transparently, collaboratively, and faster than any of us could alone.

What You're Getting

This is a complete SAP Fiori application you can run today:

Core Validation:

  • Detects encoding issues (UTF-8 compliance, BOM, null bytes)
  • Validates CSV structure (headers, delimiters, column consistency)
  • Identifies problematic content (HTML tags, entities, control characters, special characters)
  • Checks field lengths and data integrity

Auto-Correction:

  • Strips HTML tags while preserving content
  • Converts HTML entities to UTF-8 characters (  → space, ü → ü)
  • Properly escapes special characters
  • Generates a clean, download-ready corrected file

UI Features:

  • Drag-and-drop file upload
  • Interactive preview of your CSV
  • Filterable table showing issues by row and type
  • Side-by-side view of original vs. corrected content
  • One-click download of the corrected file

Privacy:

  • Everything runs locally in your browser
  • No uploads, no servers, no data leaves your machine

It's built with SAP UI5, follows SAP Fiori standards, and runs via npm. Five minutes from clone to validation.

Getting Started

git clone https://github.com/SAP/csv-validator.git
cd csv-validator
npm install
npm start

Open your browser to the app url, drop in a CSV file, and click Validate. That's it.
You can test by uploading your csv and press validate. Here is a sample screenshot of what you can expect:

 

Where We Go From Here

I have ideas for improvements—support for other SuccessFactors modules, batch processing, direct API integration. But I'm more interested in hearing yours.

If you use this tool and it helps, tell me what's missing. If it doesn't catch an issue it should, open a GitHub issue with an example. If you have a fix or enhancement, submit a pull request. If you just want to share your experience, leave a comment below.

This isn't a finished product. It's a starting point. I'm learning as much from your use cases as you might learn from the code.

An Honest Ask

If you work with SuccessFactors data imports—whether you're an administrator, consultant, or developer—try this tool with your real files. Not a sanitized demo file. Your actual data (with sensitive info removed, of course).

Then tell me:

  • Did it catch issues you would have missed?
  • Did it flag false positives that aren't actually problems?
  • What validation rule would make your life easier?

And if you've built something similar or have ideas for collaboration, let's talk. The goal isn't to centralize everything into this one tool—it's to raise the baseline for data quality across the community.

Resources:

  • GitHub Repository: github.com/SAP/csv-validator
  • Original Blog Post: Bridging Data Quality Gaps in TIH with CSVValidationApp
  • Issues & Feedback: github.com/SAP/csv-validator/issues
  • Contribution Guidelines: github.com/SAP/csv-validator/CONTRIBUTING.md

Data quality shouldn't be a bottleneck. Let's make it a strength—together.

entry.title: Assignment UUID for Deep Links in SAP SuccessFactors Latest People Profile
entry.content:
Assignment UUID for Deep Links in SAP SuccessFactors Latest People Profile

Introduction

With recent releases and the move to the Latest People Profile, many existing deep links in SAP SuccessFactors that navigate to a specific employee section or portlet in the legacy profile may no longer work as expected or may not work at all.

Previously, navigation to a specific employee was commonly done using the User ID (users-sys-id). With the latest People Profile, navigation is now based on Assignment UUID.

This is further detailed in the SAP documentation regarding deep links for profile categories and cards: Deep Links to Full Profile Categories and Cards 

Scenario

URL Format

Go to a specific category for a specific user

https://<app-server-domain>/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=<assignment UUID>&categoryId=<category Id>

This example links to a user's Job Data category:

Example

https://www.example.com/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=B2F70B823E3B4AD08091F6D1FBB74313&categoryId=jobData

If the <assignment UUID> is not specified in the URL, the deep link defaults to the signed-in user's profile rather than the intended employee's profile.

In this blog, we focus on how to navigate to a specific employee using the Assignment UUID, which is not readily available in SAP SuccessFactors and must be fetched using Integration Center for deep links.

Understanding User Identifiers in SAP SuccessFactors

SAP SuccessFactors uses multiple identifiers to represent a person and employment data in the system.

A full overview of the available Different User Identifiers in SAP SuccessFactors is available here: Different User Identifiers in SAP SuccessFactors

Assignment UUID

Field

Description

Assignment UUID

Assignment UUIDs are 32 characters and use all uppercase letters (for example: FDF7921CD3C242F59963B30E8895D04A). They are system-generated identifiers and are used in URLs and APIs without exposing personal employee data.

Can it be changed?

No

Can it be used for SSO?

No

Can it be used for integration?

Yes

Where is it displayed on People Profile?

NA

Making Assignment UUID Available for Dynamic Deep Links

The goal is to use the Assignment UUID as a dynamic token in deep links that navigate directly to an employee profile in SAP SuccessFactors.

To achieve this, the Assignment UUID must first be fetched and made available for reuse in templates or notifications. We can implement a custom solution using the Metadata Framework (MDF) and Integration Center.

Custom MDF to Store Assignment UUID

A custom MDF object is created to store the Assignment UUID. This allows the value to be reused across different use cases.

Fields include:

  • User ID
  • Assignment UUID

View of the Custom MDF object definition in Configure Object Definitions.

Integration Center Job to Populate MDF

An Integration Center job is used to fetch the Assignment UUID and populate the custom MDF object. This job should be configured to run daily to ensure that newly hired employees are included.

Integration Center field mapping, Assignment UUID mapped to the custom MDF object field.

After the Integration Center job execution, the MDF is successfully populated, as shown below. Each record now contains the corresponding Assignment UUID, ready for use in dynamic deep links.

"Manage Data" view of the custom MDF object showing records successfully populated with User IDs and their corresponding Assignment UUIDs.

Assignment UUID: 249B9F319A8B47FBA16B6537D529E7CA

Use Assignment UUID as a Dynamic Token in Deep Links

Once stored, the Assignment UUID can be used as a dynamic token in a URL in Document Generation via rule mapping, and when the notification is generated, the token is replaced dynamically.

Example deep link:

https://hcm-xxx.hr.cloud.sap/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=[[AssignmentUUID]]&categoryId=jobData

Document Generation template displaying the alert message, with the clickable link and the [[AssignmentUUID]] token shown separately to illustrate dynamic value population.

Document Generation template editor displaying the [[AssignmentUUID]] token embedded within the URL link configuration.

Document Mapping Rule (Lookup Function)

Create a rule with a Document Mapping scenario to retrieve the Assignment UUID from MDF using a lookup function.

Business Rule using a Lookup() function to fetch the Assignment UUID from the custom MDF object..

Reference Rule in Document Template Mapping

Document Generation Mapping screen where the [[AssignmentUUID]] token is assigned to the Business Rule

Notifications with Assignment UUID Deep Links

The notification now generates a dynamic link with the Assignment UUID and directs the user to the corresponding employee profile in the Latest People Profile.

The email notification: 

Email notification displaying the clickable deep link populated with the dynamic Assignment UUID.

The Link:
https://hcm-xxx.hr.cloud.sap/sf/liveprofile?assignmentId=249B9F319A8B47FBA16B6537D529E7CA&categoryId=jobData

Successful Navigation to Employee Profile

Employee profile Job Data section displayed after clicking the deep link, confirming successful navigation using the dynamic Assignment UUID.

Summary 

This blog explained how to fetch the Assignment UUID and use it in URL links to navigate to a specific employee profile, category, or card in the Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors. Since this identifier is not directly available in the system, a custom solution is implemented to retrieve and maintain it for dynamic use.

The solution is implemented as follows:

  • The Assignment UUID is retrieved using an Integration Center job and stored in a custom MDF object
  • A business rule with a lookup function dynamically fetches the value
  • The Assignment UUID is then used as a token and embedded into deep link URLs in the document generation template

Notes & Key Considerations

  • Job Frequency: Ensure your Integration Center job runs frequently enough to account for new hires.
  • URL Encoding: When building URLs, ensure there are no trailing spaces or characters that could break the string.

entry.title: Deep Dive: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland
entry.content:
Deep Dive: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland

Die Ankündigung die Gehaltsabrechnung für den deutschen Öffentlichen Dienst (ÖD) in den Lösungsumfang der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll aufzunehmen, hat eine sehr positive Resonanz bei ÖD Kunden und die DSAG hervorgerufen. Nun ist es höchste Zeit konkreter zu werden!

In diesem Blog möchten wir folgende Themen erläutern:

  1.             Was beinhaltet die Lösungsumfangserweiterung von SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP)
  2.             Ausblick für das Jahr 2026 und folgende

Was ist für ÖD Kunden eigentlich drin?

Hier empfehlen wir einen Blick auf die Länderspezifika für SF Employee Central Payroll - Öffentlicher Dienst Deutschland  zu werfen.

Zusammengefasst: der Lösungsumfang der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) enthält ab 01. Januar 2026 alle Standardfunktionalitäten für die Personalabrechnung Öffentlicher Dienst Deutschland (PY-DE-PS). Die Fakten:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll basiert auf dem SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA Stack
  • Die bekannten ÖD spezifischen Infotypen wie zB. IT0051 VBL/ZVE-Daten,  IT0509 Höherwertige Tätigkeiten,  IT0320 Dienstwohnung  IT0271 Statistiken des öffentlichen Diensts etc. sind wie gewohnt verfügbar
  • Pro Lohnart die im IT0014 Wiederkehrende Be-/Abzüge, IT0015 Ergänzende Zahlung oder IT2010 Entgeltbelege verarbeitet wird, wird definiert ob sie führend in  SF Employee Central oder in SF Employee Central Payroll gepflegt wird
  • Tarife wie zB. TVöD Bund, TVöD VKA, TVöD Pflege, TV-L etc. und ebenso die Beamtenbesoldung werden wie gewohnt abgebildet
  • ÖD spezifische Funktionalitäten wie zB. die automatische Berechnung der Urlaubs- und Krankenlohnaufschläge für unständige Bezüge, die Krankengeldzuschussberechnung, die zeitversetzte Verarbeitung der unständigen Bezüge oder die Altersteilzeit sind selbstverständlich im Standard vorhanden
  • Deutschland und ÖD spezifische Folgeaktivitäten sind im selben Umfang wie in SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA bzw. bisher in SAP ERP HCM vorhanden
  • Das PY Schema D100 für die Abrechnung ist Teil der Auslieferung
  • Kundeneigenes Schemen und Zyklen können nach SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll übernommen werden
  • Customizing Aktivitäten und kundeneigene ABAP Programmierung z.B. über BadIs oder Z-Programme stehen zur Verfügung. Siehe Hierzu empfehlen wir die Dokumentation im SAP Help Portal zum Thema Modifikation und Erweiterbarkeit von SF Employee Central Payroll. 

 

Rahmenbedingungen für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland

Die wichtigste Botschaft: Der Scope der SF Employee Central Payroll  unterscheidet sich hinsichtlich der Personalabrechnung für den Öffentlichen Dienst Deutschland inklusive Folgeaktivitäten, Auswertungen und Statistiken nicht von SAP S/4HANA. 

Natürlich gilt der allgemeine Umfang der Lösung bzw. die Rahmenbedingungen gemäß der Produktbeschreibung und der Funktionsumfangsbeschreibung für SF Employee Central Payroll auch für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland. SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll ist der Teil der SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite der für die Gehaltsabrechnung zuständig ist. Teile des HCM-Funktionsumfangs, die nicht direkt zur Gehaltsabrechnung gehören, sind wegen des starken Fokus auf Gehaltsabrechnung nicht im Lösungsumfang der EC Payroll verfügbar. Dazu gehören z.B. das Reisekostenmanagement, das Cross Application Time Sheet (CATS), die Learning Solution (LSO) oder eine Personaleinsatzplanung.

Dies vorausgeschickt möchten wir auf einige für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland wichtige Eigenheiten hinweisen. Zur besseren Lesbarkeit hier nur die zusätzlich für ÖD Kunden besonders relevanten Klarstellungen und Einschränkungen.

Kundenspezifische LösungVerwendung in Employee Central Payroll nicht erlaubt
Felder Dienstart/Unterdienstart in IT0001: Grundlegende Erweiterbarkeit: Zuordnung von Employee-Central-Feldern zu InfotypenVersorgungsadministration (VADM)
ZfA-Kommunikation über den SAP Standard hinausNachversicherungsadministration (NV)
 Organisationsmanagement Öffentliche Verwaltungen (PA-OM, PA-PM-OM)
 Stellenwirtschaft inklusive Obligoprozessor (PA-PM-CP) und Personal Budget Plan Management (PA-PM-BM)
 Reisekostenmanagement (FI-TV)
 Personaleinsatzplanung

Quelle: Länderspezifika für SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll - Öffentlicher Dienst Deutschland    Stand März 2026

Kunden aus dem Kern der öffentlichen Verwaltung z.B. aus Bund, Ländern und Kommunen, die VADM für die Versorgung Ihrer Beamten benötigen oder gesetzliche Anforderungen mit der Stellenwirtschaft befriedigen müssen, können dies nicht in der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (Stand März 2026). Weiteres dazu im Kapitel Ausblick.

Empfehlung für den Umgang mit Basisbezügen für den ÖD

Aufgrund der Verknüpfungen zu verschiedenen anderen ÖD-spezifischen Infotypen wird bis auf weiteres empfohlen, Basisbezüge (Infotyp 0008) im öffentlichen Dienst direkt in Employee Central Payroll zu verwalten und nicht aus dem Compensation Portlet in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central zu replizieren.

Dementsprechend sollten Basisbezüge (Infotyp 0008) von der Point-to-Point Replikation im Customizing (Transaktion SPRO) ausgeschlossen werden, indem Sie die beschriebenen Schritte aus der ECP Dokumentation vornehmen.

 

Voraussetzungen für SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll

Kunden die bestehendes Customizing und / oder kundeneigenen Code aus einem bestehenden SAP HCM System übernehmen möchten, können dies tun solange Sie:

  1. Funktionsumfangsbeschreibung, Produktbeschreibung und dabei insbesondere Modifikation und Erweiterbarkeit von SAP SF Employee Central Payroll beachten
  2. Sicherstellen, dass sowohl Customizing als auch kundeneigener Code mit SAP S/4HANA kompatibel sind. Beachten sie dabei insbesondere die SAP Note 3091160 - SAP HCM für SAP S/4HANA – Allgemeiner Ansatz und Strategie, welche Aufschluss über HCM Simplification Items in S/4HANA gibt

Als Startpunkt zur Identifikation von Simplification Items bietet sich der SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors HCM an. 

 

Ausblick für das Jahr 2026 und folgende

Zusammen mit unseren Kunden begeben wir uns auf die Reise dem Öffentliche Dienst in Deutschland mit der SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite moderne HCM Prozesse digital in der Cloud zur Verfügung zu stellen, ohne dabei Abstriche in Sachen Sicherheit, Souveränität und Funktionsumfang zu machen. 

SAP ist bewusst, dass besonders Kunden aus dem Kern der öffentlichen Verwaltung auf Funktionalitäten wie Versorgungsadministration oder Stellenbewirtschaftung nicht verzichten können. Zusammen mit unseren Partnern und der DSAG arbeiten wir an diesen und weiteren Themen um letztlich mit allen Kunden aus dem Öffentlichen Dienst das Ziel zu erreichen.

Es gilt der übliche Disclaimer: Dies ist natürlich keine Zusage bestimmte Funktionalitäten zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt oder überhaupt zu liefern. Die Roadmap wird sobald konkret absehbar über die dafür vorgesehen Kanäle wie Roadmap Explorer oder What's New Viewer kommuniziert.

HR souverän und sicher - SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite in der SAP Sovereign Cloud

Die Sicherheitsmaßnahmen beim Betrieb von SAP Cloud Lösungen entsprechen den höchsten Standards für Cybersicherheit, Betrieb und Datenschutz. Dies gilt insbesondere für kritische Persönliche Mitarbeiter- und Organisationsdaten in der SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite. Ausführlich informiert hier die Webseite SAP Trust Center - Sicherheit.

Zusätzliche SAP-Sovereign-Cloud-Angebote, sind eigenständige Angebote unseres Cloud-Portfolios, die auf die anspruchsvollsten Anforderungen in allen vier Dimensionen der digitalen Souveränität zugeschnitten sind: Daten, Betrieb, Recht und Technik.
Damit werden die strengeren regulatorischen und sicherheitstechnischen Anforderungen bestimmter Branchen, wie in teilen des Öffentlichen Dienstes, gefordert erfüllt.

Besonders relevant für unsere ÖD Kunden in Deutschland ist das in Deutschland gehostete Angebot SAP Cloud Infrastructure. Die Verfügbarkeit der SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite inklusive Payroll ist dort bis Ende 2026 geplant.

Weitere Informationen finden Sie unter SAP Sovereign Cloud auf www.sap.com.

 

Fazit

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll basierend auf SAP S/4HANA  und tief integriert mit SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central  ist die moderne Payroll für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland. Dabei bietet sie aus Perspektive der Gehaltsabrechnung den vollen von SAP gewohnten Leistungsumfang in einem schlanken Kosteneffizienten Cloud Deployment. SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll vereint Effektivität, Effizienz und Sicherheit/Souveränität mit einer starken AI basierten Roadmap.

SAP arbeitet zusammen mit Partnern daran jedem Kunden den Umstieg auf die Full Cloud mit SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll zu ermöglichen.

Für Informationen zur SAP HCM Strategie, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors und zu weiteren Themen, empfehlen wir einen Besuch unserer HCM Move Seite.

entry.title: Why Business Rules Become Unmanageable After Global Template Rollouts
entry.content:
Most SAP SuccessFactors implementations start with a simple goal: standardize global HR processes.
But at scale, that simplicity starts to break.

The Real Problem Isn’t Rules. It’s Scale.

Global template rollouts in SAP SuccessFactors are built on one promise:

  • One global design
  • One Employee Central template
  • Standardized HR processes

At design time, this works.

At scale, it doesn’t.

Business rules don’t break during implementation—they break during global expansion.

What starts as a clean rule framework slowly evolves into a fragmented, high-risk logic system.

The Logic Breakdown (Cause → Effect Model)

Step 1: Standardization Assumption

At the beginning, teams believe:

“We can create global rules and reuse them across all countries.”

Why this seems logical:

  • HR processes appear similar globally
  • EC data model is standardized
  • Rule framework is reusable

👉But this assumption ignores one thing: variation is inevitable

Step 2: Variation Enters the System

As rollout begins, countries introduce:

  • statutory requirements
  • local validations
  • unique job structures
  • payroll dependencies

Now the system must handle:

Global logic + Local exceptions

Step 3: The First Design Compromise

Instead of redesigning for flexibility, teams choose speed:

  • Clone existing rules
  • Add country-specific IF conditions
  • Patch exceptions into existing logic

👉This is the critical turning point

Step 4: Rule Duplication Begins

A single rule becomes multiple versions:

  • Global Rule
  • US Rule
  • Germany Rule
  • India Rule
  • APAC Variant

Why duplication happens:

  • Faster than redesign
  • Safer under deadlines
  • Easier to test locally

👉Effect: Logic is no longer centralized

Step 5: Rules Become Decision Trees

As more countries are added:

IF country = US → logic A 
IF country = DE → logic B 
IF country = IN → logic C 
ELSE → global logic

What changes:

  • Rules become longer
  • Conditions become nested
  • Readability drops

👉Effect: Rules stop being “logic” and become mini-programs

Step 6: Hidden Dependencies Emerge

Rules start interacting:

  • Rule A updates a field
  • Rule B reacts to that field
  • Rule C validates again

But no one designs this interaction intentionally.

👉Effect:

  • Unpredictable outcomes
  • Debugging becomes guesswork
  • Changes create side effects

Step 7: Governance Breaks Down

After go-live:

  • Multiple teams modify rules
  • Documentation becomes outdated
  • Ownership is unclear

Now:

  • HR modifies validations
  • IT adjusts integrations
  • Consultants patch issues

👉Effect: No single source of truth for logic

 

Step 8: Testing Complexity Explodes

With:

  • country variations
  • employee types
  • lifecycle events

The number of test scenarios grows exponentially.

👉Teams adapt by:

  • testing only main flows
  • ignoring edge cases

👉Effect: Issues move into production

 

Step 9: System Degradation (Final State)

At this stage:

You see:

  • 100–200+ rules
  • duplicated logic
  • conflicting outputs
  • slow transactions

You hear:

  • “Which rule is controlling this?”
  • “Why did this value change?”
  • “It works for US but not for Germany”

👉The system is still running—but no longer predictable

🎯The Real Root Cause

It’s not:

  • bad rules
  • bad consultants
  • bad tools

It is this assumption:

Standardization eliminates the need for flexibility design

That assumption is wrong.

✅What Should Have Happened Instead

Mature implementations design for variation from Day 1:

✔ 1. Separate Logic from Variation

  • Keep rules stable
  • Move country differences to parameters (MDF / tables)

✔ 2. Design “Variation Points”

Instead of:

  • embedding country logic inside rules

Design:

  • configurable inputs that drive behavior

✔ 3. Avoid Rule Cloning

  • One rule → multiple inputs
    NOT
  • multiple rules → same logic

✔ 4. Establish Rule Ownership

Define:

  • who creates rules
  • who modifies them
  • who retires them

✔ 5. Treat Rules as Architecture (Not Configuration)

  • version control mindset
  • periodic cleanup cycles
  • dependency awareness

Conclusion — The Core Insight

Business rules don’t become unmanageable because they were poorly designed.

They become unmanageable because:

they were not designed to handle controlled variation at scale

Final Thought

A successful global template is not one that enforces sameness.

It is one that: absorbs differences without multiplying logic

entry.title: SuccessFactors Release 1H 2026, Important notes on EC, Platform, Time Management, my view
entry.content:
First thing First 😊. Screenshots have been taken from SAP release notes only.

Latest Home Page Is Now Automatically On by Default (WEF-167105) – As per 1H 2026 release SAP has enabled the latest home page experience automatically in all systems. In case if you don’t want to adopt the latest home page make sure after release hits your production system you are turning it off in the configuration.

Deprecation of Legacy Home Page Experience (WEF-166700) – Legacy Home will be deleted on Nov 13, 2026. Customer must plan accordingly to use the latest home page. Change Management team should communicate to the end users about that. In case if you are using automated test scripts then you need plan to update the test scripts as the screen is getting changed.

Deprecation of Legacy People Profile (SFEDU-21006)– Legacy people profile will be deleted on May 15, 2026. In the meantime, latest people profile will be universally available for all customers and does not require an enablement setting. As per my testing I can see there is no major issue in the latest people profile but for Time Off Solution if the “Time Account” object is unsecured then you have to adjust a bit the configuration of Time Account Object & few changes in RBP. SAP KBA for that KBA - 3625336.

Improvements to Enhanced People Search (ISH-28758) – It’s cool. User status can be used in the filter criteria. To filter, enter keywords such as inactive David or Active David. Also, for national id You can enter the asterisk (*) as a wildcard in the National ID field for the search criteria.

Enhancements to Editing UI of Full Profile (ECT-263295) – Few screens have been modified for latest people profile along with some error message layouts have been updated.

Important observation - There is no pie chart in Compensation Information.

 

Subheading Names for the Message Popup 

Direct Workflow Approval Link in Manage Data Page (MDF-89756) – In the Manage data page workflow approval link is available which helps streamlining the process of reviewing pending or completed workflows.

Auto-Reassigning Primary Phone and Email Records (ECT-254499) - After enabling the auto-reassignment option, when you import a new primary record, the system automatically updates the existing primary record to non-primary. The import is successful and a warning message is displayed.

Old version: It showed error message.

Automated Deletion of Future-Dated Job and Compensation Information Records After Employment Termination (ECT-260432) – If this feature is enabled then all future-dated Job Information records with the relevant event reason set to delete are automatically removed during the Terminate action, regardless of how the records were created.

In the earlier version, even after the termination the future dated Job & Compensation Information records remained in the system after the termination which required manual deletion.

Workflow Participant Information Available in Mobile Apps (MOB-107874) - Cool one. In the mobile app user can view the with whom the request is pending for approval. The feature applies to Employee Central, MDF, Time Off, and Time Sheet workflows.

 

 

Enhancements to Time Off and Time Sheet Custom Workflow Notifications (TIM-52812) – Nice one. Custom workflow notification text can be used for Time Off, Time Sheet and Single Record Approvals through the document generation mechanism. Following dynamic fields are supported.

  • Employee name
  • Time type recorded by the employee
  • Start Time/Start Date and End Time/End Date of the absence or attendance request

entry.title: A small config change that saved my HR users a lot of headache — Fuzzy Search in SuccessFactors EC
entry.content:
Introduction

During one of my EC implementations in SAU project, I kept hearing the same complaint from HR users: they couldn't find employees through global search. A small issue on the surface, but it was slowing down daily work for the entire HR team. This post covers what was causing it and the one setting that fixed it.

What was happening

We were working with a client that had a large Arabic-speaking workforce and one thing you quickly notice in that context is that Arabic names transliterated into English don't have one fixed spelling. Mohammed, Muhammad, Mohamed all the same name, all spelled differently depending on who filled in the form.

HR users were complaining that they couldn't find employees through global search. My first reaction honestly was are they just not typing the name correctly? But after connected with a few of them and watching how they searched, I realized the problem wasn't them at all.

They were doing things like:

  • Typing Muhamed instead of Mohammed
  • Searching with a partial name like Fatim instead of Fatima
  • Small typos like Khaleed instead of Khalid 

and in all these cases the system just returned nothing, or completely irrelevant results. So they'd try again, rephrase, try again and wasting time on something that should take 5 seconds.

The fix — enabling Fuzzy Search

Fuzzy Search allows the system to return close matches even when the input is not exact. It handles spelling variations, partial names, and minor typos. Once I enabled it, the results changed immediately.

 

 

Before:

✗Muhamed → no results
✗Fatim → nothing useful
✗Khaleed → search fails

After:

✓Muhamed → suggests Mohamed
✓Fatim → returns Fatima
✓Khaleed → suggests Khalid
 

 

How to enable it

Simple Admin Center config — took me less than 2 minutes:

  1. Go to Admin Center
  2. Click on Tools
  3. Search for Company System and Logo Settings
  4. Enable "Fuzzy Search for People Search"

 Save Company System Settings

⏱Give it about 10 minutes to kick in

A few practical notes

One thing I noticed during testing Fuzzy Search works specifically in the Global Header People Search. It is not a system wide setting, so do not expect it to fix search issues in People Profile or the Employee Directory. Those areas work differently and have their own separate permissions and settings. If users are still complaining about search in those areas after you enable this, that is a separate investigation.

 

Conclusion

This experience was a good reminder that not every user complaint points to a training gap. Sometimes, the system simply is not configured to match real world usage.

In regions like Saudi Arabia, where Arabic name transliterations can vary significantly, enabling Fuzzy Search is not just a nice to have. It is practically essential for effective People Search.

If you are implementing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central for a multilingual or Arabic speaking workforce and have not reviewed this setting yet, it is definitely worth checking. It is a quick, 2 minute configuration change that can greatly improve everyday usability.

And the next time a search issue is raised, take a moment to review the system configuration before assuming it is a user error. It might just save you from drawing the wrong conclusion.

entry.title: The New Look of Success: Navigating the 2026 Experience Updates
entry.content:
We are in the midst of a major evolution for the SuccessFactors experience. Targeting three main areas: the Latest Home Page, the Latest People Profile, and the New Edit/History UI, these changes are designed to make the system feel less like a technical database and more like the modern, intuitive apps we use every day. While these changes may feel daunting, it opens the doors to de-clutter processes and move towards a people-first experience. 

Let’s dive into what’s changing, what we’re excited about, and most importantly, how to prepare for the shift!

The Latest Home Page: Your New Command Center

With the changes coming to the home page, it will feel more than a static landing page, but rather a dynamic launchpad. It is cleaner and more concise, focusing on exactly what needs to be done now.


Some Key Latest Home Page Features:

  • Quick Actions: The time savers of the homepage. Common transactions can be made directly from the homepage in the new quick-actions header, while navigation to specific pages can be done at the click of a button from the quick-links section. There can be up to 14 custom actions and links activated at once, making the day-to-day transactions of a business feel instant.
  • Personal Customization :Employees can now feel a sense of ownership over their digital workspace by having the option to choose which quick actions or quick links they want to show on their home page. If an employee prefers a cleaner look, they can remove their name from the greeting message as well - a small touch to add a bigger, personal feel.
  • Needs Attention Section:All home page cards that require attention are displayed together in one section rather than being broken up, reducing the ‘scroll fatigue’ that came from locating a task notification. These cards can be filtered based by category and type, making navigation a breeze.
  • Organizational Updates: This section of the homepage will retain its standard functionality - providing teams with a space to post about company news & custom content. All of these can be grouped into separate tabs (folders), keeping the page concise while ensuring no important news is missed

Some items to keep in mind:

  • Personalization Guardrails: Quick actions can be made un-hideable by the system admin, ensuring employees don't accidentally hide essential HR tools they actually need
  • Layout Changes: Some buttons/items will now be found in different areas of the homepage. For example, the Team Absence Calendar has shifted from ‘Quick Actions’ over to ‘Quick Links.’
  • Branding Changes: While the traditional banner image has been removed in the latest experience, a new setting ‘Brand Image’ has been added - where the applied image appears on the greeting banner of the homepage
  • Reduced Navigation Option: Quick actions that were already in the main home page drop down (like the Org Chart) have been removed to reduce redundant navigation
  • Filtering of Tasks: The way filters work for tasks in the 'Needs Attention' section actually correspond to how cards were organized in the legacy experience.
  • Custom Tiles: Your old tiles might look a bit different in the new layout. It's a good time to check if the images still fit and if the text used is still relevant
  • Theming: The new home page uses more white space, so your corporate colors might need a slight tweak in your theme(s) to ensure everything flows and fits the companies branding style

Reminder - The Latest Home Page update is currently being turned on in preview environments by default - making now the perfect time to familiarize yourself with your customization options, and get a feel for the flow before it becomes the standard.

The Latest People Profile: Spotlight on Your People

With the update to the people profile, we are moving away from the long-page scrolling view to a sleek, card-based interface. The two distinct areas of updates that are brought in with this new experience are the Profile Preview & Spotlight and the Full Profile. 

 Some Key Spotlight Features:

  • Growth Portfolio Integration: A centralized view of an employee's skills, competencies, and attributes. If enabled, it brings the Talent Intelligence Hub directly into the profile
  • Career Development Integration: Highlights future-focused data like target roles and development goals. This will help target focus towards career growth for managers & their direct reports
  • Organization Information: A simplified header that provides instant context on where the person sits in the hierarchy, including their manager and location, without needing to click into the Org Chart
  • Badges: Employees can see their earned badges instantly, boosting engagement and peer-to-peer appreciation

Some Key Profile Preview Features

  • Quick Actions: Perform some common tasks directly from the spotlight without navigating away from the current page

Some Key Full Profile Features:

  • Categories & Cards: Data is now organized into logical 'Cards' (ex. Personal Information, Payment Information - referred to previously as Portlets) grouped under 'Categories'. This eliminates the infinite scroll layout and allows users to jump directly to the data they need
  • Centralized Permission Control: Admins can now control the visibility of categories & cards within through the 'Configure Latest People Profile' tool. By default, all categories are made visible to everyone or you can choose to specify the permission roles you want to grant access for. From there, the roles can be updated via the 'Permission Roles' tool for more updates, ensuring data privacy is maintained
  • Direct Reports & Team Size View: On a manager profile, you can now view the number of employees who directly report to the manager and the size of the manager's team. This feature is available to be displayed on the Full Profile header.
  • Profile Header Fields: You can now add up to three fields to Full Profile header, allowing you to display additional employee information
  • Actions & Links: Through the actions icon, users will have access to a list of actions & links divided into four groups: Change, View, Take Action, and Links

Some items to keep in mind:

  • Card Organization Blocks: Predefined cards are now locked to specific categories. For example - an employees Payment Information is locked to the Personal Information category of the profile
  • Permission Control: Some fields that were visible in the legacy view may require a quick RBP (Role-Based Permission) check to ensure they appear correctly in the new card-based layout
  • Block Descriptions: The feature to add a block description for custom cards has now been moved to ‘Help Text’, which can be used for all Full Profile cards

The New Edit & History UI: Precision and Transparency

Building on the Full Profile update, the New Edit/History UI replaces the old pop-up windows with streamlined experience. The focus here is on transparency, giving users a clear view of where a record has been and where it is going. This feature is available only for customers who are currently using or are planning to implement Employee Central.

Some Key Features

  • Field Masking: Previously, masked fields were often revealed during the edit process. In the new UI, sensitive values remain masked by default even while editing, ensuring privacy of employee data in all areas
  • Visual Change Tracking: With the previous edit UI, if a field was automatically updated due to onChange rules or associations, there was no clear indicator of this additional change. Now, the changed field will be highlighted in blue, with a pop-up message informing the user that the field was changed based on another field's change
  • Accessible Save Button: Previously, the Save button only enabled when a change was performed. This has been updated to be permanently active, with the system informing you that your record contains no modifications if true upon clicking
  • Consistent Experience: Whether you are updating a phone number, a dependent, or job information, the UI looks and behaves exactly the same way
  • Workflow Clarity: The New Edit UI is fully integrated with Workflows. If a change requires approval, the UI clearly indicates that the "Change is Pending Approval" directly on the card.

Some items to keep in mind:

  • Field Grouping & Logic: With the legacy Configure People Profile admin tool, you were able to organize how fields are displayed on the Editing UI of specific Employee Central blocks. With the new UI, fields will appear in the same order on the Edit UI as they do on the View mode of the portlet, which is based on the data model configuration (BCUI)
  • Field Masking: If you choose to unmask a field while making edits to a record, you won't be able to re-mask it during that specific editing session
  • Shift in Validation Logic: Error messages triggered by onChange rules will now be displayed as warning messages, allowing users to proceed on the UI. To trigger error messages that prevent saving, you will now need to have a distinct onSave rule applied

The Switch: How to Prepare

To ensure a smooth transition to these new experiences, here are a few items to review with your teams for alignment

  • Documentation: Update your internal "How-To" guides. Screenshots of the old pop-up windows will quickly look outdated once the new side-panel UI is enabled
  • Data Accuracy: Since the new UI has stricter validations, it might surface legacy data errors that the old UI ignored. Use this as an opportunity for a "data spring cleaning”
  • Audit Your Permissions (RBP): Since the new profile changes how data is displayed, double-check your Role-Based Permissions to ensure "Confidential" fields stay confidential in the new card views
  • Business Configuration (BCUI) Cleanup: Since the New Edit UI will now display fields following the BCUI layout, take some time to review your Data Model and ensure the most important fields are at the top of each element so users don't have to hunt for them in the new card layout
  • Validate Business Rules: Since onChange rules now behave as warnings in the new UI, now is the time to review your validations. If you have a "must-have" validation that should prevent a user from saving incorrect data, ensure you have a corresponding onSave rule
  • Mobile App Review: The Latest People Profile and Home Page are designed to be mobile-first. If your organization uses the SuccessFactors Mobile App, test these changes on a mobile device. Ensure that your custom quick actions and card layouts translate well to a smaller screen to provide a consistent experience across all platforms
  • Theme Manager Review: The new visual theme introduces more white space and specific card styling. Check your Theme Manager settings to ensure your company’s primary brand colors provide enough contrast against the new card backgrounds. You may find that buttons or links need a slight color tweak to remain accessible and stand out against the cleaner layout

While change can feel like a lot of moving parts, these updates are a large step towards a modern, intuitive HR ecosystem. With these changes, you'll be using a more empowered and efficient way of working within your SuccessFactors Environment.

entry.title: First Half 2026 Release: What’s new in SAP SuccessFactors solutions for workforce management
entry.content:
The first half 2026 release of SAP SuccessFactors solutions for workforce management brings a wave of innovation across time tracking, absence management, and workforce scheduling. From AI-powered pay transparency to intelligent shift planning, these updates help organizations manage their workforce with greater precision, compliance, and efficiency. In this blog, I’ll walk through the key highlights across SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking, Employee Central Time Management, and Workforce Scheduling. For a full overview, be sure to check out the What’s New Viewer.

Closing the loop between time valuation and pay transparency

One of the standout additions in this release is the integration of time sheet data into the Payroll Explanation Agent. Employees can now receive clear, step-by-step explanations of how their recorded working time, overtime, and premiums are valued and how these valuations directly affect their pay. By bringing together time and payroll data into a single natural language experience, the agent removes the complexity behind pay calculations and puts actionable insight directly in the hands of employees.

This capability strengthens trust between employees and their organizations. Rather than relying on HR to interpret payslips or resolve time-and-pay discrepancies, employees get fast, accurate answers about how their time entries translate into earnings. The result is less administrative overhead for HR and a more informed, empowered workforce.

Payroll Explanation Agent with time sheet data

Strengthening compliance and overtime control

This release also introduces attendance quotas, giving organizations tighter control of overtime recording. Employees can record overtime or other quotas within predefined, approved limits, and the system automatically handles situations where no pre-approval exists, whether that means triggering alerts or posting automatically to flextime accounts.

For organizations managing complex labor policies or operating in regulated industries, this capability helps prevent unauthorized overtime postings, supports budget control, and ensures that time recording remains compliant without requiring constant manual oversight. Read more here: Managing Pre-approvals using Attendance Quota

Attendance quotas

Resolving time conflicts automatically

Managing overlaps between absences and attendances has long been a time-consuming task for time administrators. The enhanced collision handling capability in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking addresses this directly. When a collision is detected, the system automatically resolves the overlap and surfaces a clear warning message on the time sheet, so employees can verify and take action if needed.

This update provides greater flexibility with enhanced collision configuration options, eliminates the need for manual record adjustments in situations where corrections would otherwise be overwhelming, and supports compliance with regulatory requirements. Read more here: Collision Handling between Absences and Attendances in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking

Collision handling

Expanding payroll integration options

For organizations using external payroll providers, this release introduces structured guidance and tooling for 3rd party payroll integration with SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking. This includes best practices for extracting, formatting, and transmitting time tracking data, including absences, time valuation results, payouts, and time accounts, to external payroll systems.

The integration leverages a new API for replication confirmation to third-party payroll systems, ensuring accurate handling of retroactive changes. The outcome is enhanced payroll accuracy, reduced maintenance effort, and stronger compliance with organizational and regulatory requirements. Read more here: Extending Data Replication Proxies to Third-Party Payroll Systems

3rd Party Payroll Integration

Advancing time recording for public sector

For customers running SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking alongside SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, this release delivers consolidated time recording enhancements for the public sector. These updates provide access to actual costs based on time valuation results within funds and grants management reporting, addressing a critical requirement for regulated public sector organizations.

Employees can now record time entries using alternative cost objects such as funds, grants, fund centers, and WBS elements, not just cost centers. This enables regulated industries to report actual costs rather than planned costs using payroll-processed time data, which is essential for compliance with funds and grants management regulations.

Deeper absence insights with People Intelligence

This release brings a dedicated Time Management Insights app as part of People Intelligence in SAP Business Data Cloud. HR teams now have a single, comprehensive view of workforce absence analytics, including absence duration and occurrence data, unscheduled absence and sick leave tracking, and absence rates with contributing factors.

By identifying absence patterns and trends early, organizations can take proactive action to reduce absenteeism, improve resource planning, and evaluate the impact of health and wellness initiatives. This capability enables both internal benchmarking and external comparisons, giving HR leaders the data they need to strengthen overall workforce strategy.

Time Management Insights

SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Scheduling

Following its early adopter launch in December 2025, SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Scheduling is planned for general availability this July. This release delivers two foundational capabilities that bring workforce planning and execution together in a single, streamlined experience.

Demand definition allows planners to define labor needs by work center and shift, specifying required headcount, skills, and utilization factors. A consolidated view of planned headcount, planned hours, and total shifts gives planners complete visibility into coverage requirements, with mass edit capabilities and full customization for each shift and work center.

The new shift scheduling board with shift proposals takes this further by generating system-driven shift proposals based on the defined demand. These proposals factor in required skills, employee absences, public holidays, and planned working hours, allowing planners to validate and publish more efficiently.

New shift board (lab preview)

 

Together, these features help organizations right-size coverage, protect service levels, control labor costs, and keep schedules aligned and compliant across locations. For manufacturing and production environments where scheduling precision directly impacts output, this is a significant step forward.

Explore What’s New

You can explore all the new enhancements for SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking, Employee Central Time Management. Information about SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Scheduling will be available at the planned GA in July 2026. For additional release information and assets, visit the SAP SuccessFactors community page.

entry.title: 1H 2026 SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors: Connected Solutions and New AI-First Capabilities
entry.content:
With the 1H 2026 release, we have delivered new capabilities within SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors to improve how organizations intelligently attract and hire the best talent.  

To learn more watch the on-demand Q&A webinars: 1H 2026 Product Release Webinars and Recruiting & Onboarding 1H 2026 Customer Release Q&A. To hear more about the roadmap and broader view of our strategy, watch the 1H 2026 VIP Virtual Program for Strategic Roadmap Updates.    

All of the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 release items can be found on the What’s New Viewer. These new features will be live in Production on May 15th, 2026.

With the 1H 2026 release, we have delivered a deeper integration between SAP SuccessFactors HCM and SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors as well as updates to Winston, SmartRecruiters’ suite of AI products, which are now available for SAP SuccessFactors customers.

Our integration with SmartRecruiters allows for more consistency and synergy across both products and allows our customers to take advantage of the latest recruiting AI in candidate conversion and team engagement.

Now let’s explore the new features, including a deeper integration between SAP SuccessFactors and SmartRecruiters as well as updates to Winston products such as Companion, Chat, and Match.

SmartRecruiters & SAP SuccessFactors Integration

SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors now have a native integration with HCM sync and SSO login. That means a seamless, unified experience across both ecosystems with easy connection to Employee Central and Onboarding modules.

With these solutions connected, you can look forward to greater operational efficiency that eliminates the need to manually enter data or replicate work.

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors Launch

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors is now fully available for SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting customers, bringing conversational AI capabilities into your current solution to elevate candidate engagement and experiences.

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors runs on your career site to help recruiting teams engage candidates at scale with generative AI, from job discovery and applications to screening and interview scheduling.

The entire candidate journey is delivered conversationally, so every action is as easy as sending a message to a friend. For example, a candidate looking for a job could simply ask Winston ‘do you have any sales jobs in Seattle?’ and receive an answer directly in the chat.

Look forward to boosting candidate conversion and reducing administrative work with these feature highlights:

  • Chat available 24/7
  • Customizable avatar, colors, and tone of voice
  • Conversational job discovery, applications, and screening questions
  • Automated interview scheduling based on criteria like answers to screening questions.
  • Dashboard to review active bots and performance metrics

Winston Companion: Q&A Agent

Delivered within the core SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors solution, customers benefit from Winston’s enhanced AI capabilities that streamline recruiting processes and drive better business outcomes. Winston Companion’s Q&A agent is your hiring team’s one-stop shop for instant, trustworthy recruiting system and process answers. Users can ask questions without leaving their workflow, rather than opening new tabs and manually looking through article after article.

This convenient new feature helps recruiters save time and stay on task with the following feature highlights:

  • Ask questions about the recruiting process or system directly in Winston Companion.
  • Get instant, contextualized answers with link references to ensure accuracy and allow you to dive deeper.

Winston Companion: Auto Self-Scheduling

Winston Companion’s Auto Self-Scheduling gives hirers a fast, low‑touch way to move qualified candidates from shortlist to confirmed interviews, without the usual back‑and‑forth over email or chat.

By combining preconfigured interview templates, calendar availability, and automation rules, Winston Companion can trigger one‑click scheduling flows directly from a conversational prompt, whether a hirer is working in the web app or via mobile channels like WhatsApp.

Look forward to increased efficiency with the following key features:

  • Template‑driven automation that uses existing interview templates and self-scheduling rules to send out accurate, consistent invites
  • Bulk invitations for high‑volume roles

Winston Match: Subscores

Winston Match subscores give recruiters a holistic, nuanced view of how each candidate’s skills, experience, and education contribute to their overall Match Score. Instead of a single aggregate number, subscores expose the underlying signals Winston uses to evaluate fit, so teams can see not just who is ranked highly, but why they’re ranked that way.

For high-volume screening, subscores make Winston Match insights instantly usable in list views. This accelerates shortlisting while keeping recruiters firmly in control of who moves forward through the following key features.

  • Subscore dimensions of skills, experience, and education/certifications
  • Easy overview of subscores in the applicant list
  • Flexible UI allows users to hide the subscore column if not needed

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors: Search Jobs via Resume with Winston Chat

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors: Search Jobs via Resume makes job discovery as simple as dropping a CV into the conversation through Winston Chat. Instead of typing keywords or scrolling through pages of listings, candidates upload their resume and let Winston Chat surface relevant roles automatically.

Enable recruiters to reduce friction in the application process while also matching candidates to the most relevant roles, so you can look forward to a boost in high-quality candidate conversion.

Continuous Innovation

At SAP, our goal with each release is to make the recruiting process more efficient and consistent while adding a touch of joy along the way. We want to empower hirers to take time for strategic tasks and meaningful communication that makes your team more visible and valued.

That’s why we are so excited about this round of updates. With the enhanced suite of Winston capabilities, recruiters can leverage generative AI directly within their workflow to boost the metrics that matter to them, like candidate conversion and time to hire, whether that means leveraging Winston Match subscores or SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors automated scheduling.

With every release, we are excited to deliver these new innovations and continue our journey of providing even greater value to our customers. For full details, please check out the 1H 2026 Release Highlights Resources: 

  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Brochure
  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Video 
  • Product Release & Road Map Information

Learn more about SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors.

entry.title: Automating Probation Extensions After Sick Leave in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Introduction

In many organizations, the probation period is designed to give both the employer and the employee a fair window to assess performance, cultural fit, and overall suitability for the role. But what happens when that evaluation period is interrupted? Employee absences, especially due to short term sickness, can significantly reduce the time available for meaningful observation, leaving managers with incomplete information at the end of the probation timeline.

To address this, some companies adopt a policy of extending the probation period by the exact number of days an employee was absent during that time. While the intent is straightforward, the execution can quickly become complex. Tracking absence days, recalculating end dates, and ensuring consistency across the organization often leads to manual effort, errors, and compliance risks.

This is where SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) can play a crucial role. By leveraging automation and business rules, organizations can seamlessly adjust probation end dates based on recorded absences, ensuring fairness, accuracy, and operational efficiency. In this blog, we’ll explore how to design and implement an automated approach to extending probation periods when short term sick leave occurs, helping HR teams move from manual tracking to a more intelligent, system-driven process.

Proposed Solution

The proposed solution is based on standard SuccessFactors functionalities, namely Intelligent Services Center (ISC), Integration Center (IC), and Business Rules.

In this scenario, whenever a short-term sickness absence is recorded, an Employee Time event triggers a process to evaluate whether the employee is still within the probation period and, if so, automatically extend the probation end date in the Job Information portlet.

Below is a step-by-step guide to building it.

Step 1: Navigate to Integration Center and click “My Integrations” to start creating your IC job.

Step 2: Now click on “+ Create” and select “More Integration Types”.

Step 3: In this step, you need to define the integration type. For this scenario, select the options as shown in the screenshot below. Once selected, click “Create.”

Step 4: On the first screen of the IC job, you are required to provide a name, a description, and a page size. Below is an example from a demo system. Once these fields are defined, click “Next.”

Step 5: Next, navigate to the “Configure Fields” tab and, from the menu in the top-right corner, select the “Switch to Field Mapping View” option.

On this page, we first define the destination table, for this scenario, we use “Job Information.”

Next, we configure the mapping from the “Employee Time” table to Job Information. As shown in the screenshot below, only two fields are directly mapped: “Event Date,” which is mapped to the absence “End Date,” and “User ID,” mapped to the “User ID” from the source table.

These mappings are created easily by dragging and dropping fields from the source table on the left to the corresponding fields in the destination table on the right.

Step 6: Next, switch to “Detail View” using the option in the top-right menu.

We will now define fields that do not have a direct mapping but instead require a default value. To do this, locate the “Sequence Number” field in the table on the right. Select it, then in the left panel assign a value greater than 2, for example, 9.

Setting a number higher than 2 helps prevent potential errors. If, on the same day, the job attempts to create a new time slice in Job Information while another record already exists, the system will use this higher value to generate an additional sequence number. It’s important to note that the final sequence may not necessarily be 9, as the system intelligently determines the correct sequence based on existing records.

Now we complete the second action in this step by assigning a default value to the “Event Reason” field for this scenario.

Note: It is recommended to create a dedicated event reason for this use case to avoid unintentionally triggering the business rule (which we will configure later) in other situations.

Step 7: We have now completed the creation of our IC job, so click “Save” as shown in the screenshot.

Step 8: In this step, we will assign the IC job we created to the ISC. Navigate to the Intelligent Services Center page, search for the event “Employee Short Term Disability,” and click to open it.

Step 9: Create a new flow if one already exists for this event. Click “Integration” from the right-hand menu, which will add a new line in the center of the page. Set the “Timing” to “When event is published.” Then, use the three-dot menu (highlighted in green) to save your changes.

Note: It is also advisable to create an ISC rule that limits the process to employees whose probation status is neither completed nor failed (or based on any other criteria relevant to your organization).

Step 10: In this step, we will create our first business rule, which will trigger the Intelligent Services Center event. To do this, navigate to the “Configure Object Definition” transaction and select the “Employee Time” object.

Click to create a new rule, following the example below. Once the rule is created, assign it on Save in the object and then save the object.

Note: The absence type used in the rule example is for demonstration purposes only. Please ensure you use the absence type relevant to your organization.

Step 11: Our final step is to create a business rule that calculates the new probation end date considering the number of days the employee was on sick leave.

To do this, navigate to “Manage Business Configuration” > “Job Information.” In edit mode, scroll to the bottom of the page. On the last line, click to create a new business rule as shown below. Once the rule is created, assign it to the onSave event in the portlet and save your changes.

Conclusion

By combining Intelligent Services Center (ISC), Integration Center (IC), and Business Rules, we have demonstrated how SAP SuccessFactors can be configured to automate the extension of probation periods based on employee sick leave. This approach ensures that any absence occurring during the probation period is accurately reflected by extending the end date accordingly, eliminating the need for manual adjustments.

Beyond improving administrative efficiency, this solution also enhances fairness and consistency in HR processes, ensuring that all employees are evaluated over an equivalent active working period. It reduces the risk of human error, strengthens compliance with internal policies, and provides a scalable framework that can be adapted to other absence-driven scenarios.

Finally, this use case illustrates the power of SAP SuccessFactors standard tools when combined thoughtfully. With the right configuration, organizations can transform complex HR requirements into automated, transparent, and maintainable processes that support both HR teams and employees alike.

I hope this solution helps you improve your Employee Central setup. Feel free to reach out or comment if you have questions, or if you’d like to share how you’ve tackled probation extensions due to employees absences in your own environment.

entry.title: Turning “Moments of truth” into lasting impressions with SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
"Moments of truth" in Onboarding

Admittedly, it has been a while since I studied Service Management at business school, and many of the theories date back to the 1980s. Still, one idea has always stayed with me: people judge experiences by comparing their expectations with their perception. In short, this is how we assess an experience. These are often referred to as “Moments of Truth.”

Even today, this concept remains highly relevant, if you ask me.

In the employee journey, one of the most important moments (of truth) happens after the contract is signed, when preboarding and onboarding begin. This is where expectations are tested, and first impressions are formed through communication, preparation, and early interactions.

It is both emotional and practical, and these first impressions tend to last.

Across industries, a common pattern is that organizations invest heavily in attracting talent, but far less in structuring the onboarding experience from “offer signed” to day one. People and relationships remain essential, but without structure and process support, the experience often becomes inconsistent.

This is where SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding can support organizations by enabling a more consistent, structured, and scalable onboarding experience. If it is used to its full extend.

 

From candidate to employee - without friction

At its core, SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding connects what is typically disconnected. It brings together systems, processes, and people into one experience.

Instead of handing a new hire over from recruiting into emails, PDFs, and spreadsheets, everything continues in one flow. The candidate becomes a new hire, and then an employee, without changing platform or experience.

The solution supports onboarding, cross-boarding, offboarding, and rehire scenarios in one place. This reflects the reality that employee journeys are not always linear.

In many implementations, the value of having one centralized experience is initially underestimated. In practice, it significantly reduces friction and manual coordination.

 

A guided, structured onboarding experience that sets people up for success

The system is designed around guided processes. New hires are not expected to figure things out themselves. Instead, they are supported step by step through tasks such as data collection, document handling, learning, and introductions.


 

Before day one, employees can access a personalized homepage to complete forms and prepare. On day one, the focus shifts to connections, clarity, and productivity. Activities such as buddy assignments, meetings, and onboarding tasks are already planned.

This creates a more consistent onboarding experience while still allowing flexibility for different roles and local requirements.

From a process perspective, this reduces waiting time and helps employees become productive earlier.

 

Seamless onboarding and data flow across the SuccessFactors suite

One of the most powerful aspects of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding is its native connection to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central.

This is not a traditional integration. Data entered during onboarding is written directly into Employee Central. There is no duplication, no interfaces, and no need to re-enter information. From day one, you have one consistent and reliable data foundation.

What makes this particularly strong is that it applies to all actors involved in the process. The new hire, even before officially starting and while still an external user, can access a secure part of the system and enter their own data directly into the core HR system. The same applies to hiring managers and HR, who can update or enrich data during onboarding. Everything is written to the same place, in real time.

This has a clear practical impact. It reduces errors, removes manual steps, and ensures that employee data is available immediately for downstream processes.
